themes.exampleQuote: One of the widest tea selections I have ever seen
a wonderful pseudo-third space where you can sip tea, relax, play a board game, study, and talk to other patrons
Staff is extremely friendly and knowledgeable about tea
It's hard to get a seat sometimes because it's crowded

notable_quote: One of my favorite places on Earth: a wonderfully large selection of teas from around the world, served in an intimate and quiet setting.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently praise the depth of the tea menu, the unhurried atmosphere, and a staff that can actually explain what they're serving; the only recurring friction is seating availability during peak hours.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-eight Pittsburgh creators filmed Dobra Tea this quarter, generating more than 2.6 million TikTok plays, while the business posted once to Instagram in the same thirty days and has no TikTok presence of its own. The gap is stark: the city has decided this place is worth showing to millions of people, and Dobra is watching from the sidelines. In the Pittsburgh Cafes family, Allegheny Coffee and Tea Exchange leads the issue on social momentum, a signal Dobra could realistically contest given the creator heat already surrounding it. Closing the distance to that top spot in the broader café ranking depends almost entirely on whether the Instagram account wakes up and whether Dobra finds a way to meet the creators who are already doing the work.
tldr_read: Dominant review sentiment and 2.6 million TikTok plays from other people's cameras, one Instagram post in thirty days. Ones to Watch, #1 in Pittsburgh Tea Houses.
tldr_meaning: The review floor is exceptionally high and creator interest is not a question, the rank ceiling is determined entirely by how quickly the Instagram signal recovers and whether the business establishes any owned presence on the platform driving all those views.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: First In Tea, Built On 1,107 Reviews. The Camera Belongs To Everyone Else.

playbook.headline: Post to Instagram before the week ends
playbook.action: The account went 18 days between posts this period; one post per week is the minimum to hold algorithmic distribution at a 2.76% engagement rate.

playbook.headline: Acknowledge the 28 creators already filming
playbook.action: Twenty-eight creators drove 2.6 million plays this quarter with zero coordination; a simple repost or story reshare starts building a feedback loop Dobra currently has no part in.

playbook.headline: Tag food categories consistently in new photos
playbook.action: With 690 Google photos across only six categories, dedicated tagging of the snack platters, sesame cookies, and baba ganoush reviewers name specifically would deepen the visual catalog beyond its current above-median position.
